title: Pinalia nilgherensis
taxonomy: Pinalia nilgherensis is classified within the plant family Orchidaceae. This family consists of approximately 30,000 species of orchids, which are one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. Pinalia nilgherensis is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae, and tribe Vandeae. The species is a member of the genus Pinalia, which is a group of orchids native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Pinalia nilgherensis is subject to ongoing research and revisions in the field of botany.
uses: Pinalia nilgherensis is a plant species known for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its uses. constituents: Pinalia nilgherensis contains various active constituents, including alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides. intro: Pinalia nilgherensis, also known as Nilgiri orchid or Nilgiri Pinalia, is a species of orchid native to the Western Ghats of India. The health benefits of Pinalia nilgherensis include its potential use in managing blood sugar levels and as an anti-inflammatory agent, due to its ability to lower oxidative stress and improve insulin sensitivity. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Pinalia nilgherensis is commonly prepared as a powder or decoction, often used in traditional Indian medicine to treat various health conditions, including diabetes and respiratory issues.
